seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Smithfield Plains and Smoky Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Smithfield Plains vs Smoky Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Smithfield Plains and Smoky Bay.
heading: Smithfield Plains vs Smoky Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Smithfield Plains, South Australia and Smoky Bay, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Smoky Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Smithfield Plains (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Smithfield Plains or Smoky Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Smithfield Plains has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Smoky Bay scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Smithfield Plains scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Smoky Bay scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Smithfield Plains or Smoky Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Smoky Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
